coredata.dc:title: NiMnO3 nanoparticles anchored on graphene quantum dot: Application in sensitive electroanalysis of dobutamine
coredata.dc:creator: Hasanpour, Foroozan
Nekoeinia, Mohsen
Semnani, Abolfazl
Shojaei, Sahar
coredata.dc:description: Simple hydrothermal route was developed for synthesis of NiMnO3 in the presence of graphene quantum dot. The structure and morphological aspect of nano-hybrid were approved using FE-SEM, XRD and FT-IR. Then, these nano-particles were chosen as a modifier in carbon paste electrode, which produces noticeable catalytic effect for electro-oxidation of dobutamine. The oxidation mechanism of the drug was also studied using cyclic voltammetry. After variable optimization, a linear regression function was found in the range of 0.08–40.0 μM with the detection limit of 0.02 μM between the concentration of dobutamine and its anodic signal. The utility of the modified electrode was assessed by measuring the concentration of dobutamine in serum samples.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Dobutamine
Graphene quantum dot
NiMnO3 nanoparticles
Modified carbon paste electrode
Electroanalysis

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Determination of DB in spiked serum

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: FE-SEM image of NiMnO3 NPs (A) and GQDs/NiMnO3 (B); XRD patterns of the synthesized GQDs/NiMnO3 (C); FTIR spectra of NiMnO3 NPs (D) and QDs/NiMnO3 (E).
CVs of 3.0 mM [Fe(CN)6]3−/4− at CPE (A); NiMnO3/CPE (B) and GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE (C).
CVs for oxidation current of 5.0 μM DB at (a) CPE; (b) NiMnO3/CPE; and (c) GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE; CVs of the blank solution at: (d) CPE, (e) NiMnO3/CPE and (f) GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE.
CVs of 3.0 μM DB at the GQDs/NiMnO3 in pH 3, 5 and 7 of PBS; scan rate 100 mV s−1.
Cyclic voltammograms of 2.0 μmol L−1 DB at GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE with various scan rates as: a) 10; b) 20; c) 30; d) 50, e) 70, f) 90, g) 100; and e) 120 mV s−1.
DPVs for different concentrations of dobutamine in pH 3.0. PBS at GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE. Concentrations of dobutamine from (a) to (k): 0, 0.08, 1.5, 5.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0, 25.0, 30.0, 35.0, 40.0 μmol L−1.
Comparison of detection limit and linear range of the proposed method with recently published voltammetric methods for the determination of DB.

ce:legend.ce:simple-para: TiO2/N/CNP/GCE: nano-TiO2/nafion/carbon nanoparticles modified glassy carbon electrode; MWCNTPE: Multiwall Carbon Nanotubes Paste Electrode; AuCoPcCPE: gold nanoparticles/cobaltphthalo cyanine modified carbon paste electrode GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE: graphene quantum dots/NiMnO3 nanohybrid particles/carbon paste electrode.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Maximum tolerable concentration of interfering species for DB concentration of 5.0 μmol L−1.

row.entry: Concentration ratio of the substance to noscapine (mol/mol)

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Determination of DB in spiked serum samples.

row.entry: Found by proposed method a (μmol L−1)

head.ce:title: NiMnO3 nanoparticles anchored on graphene quantum dot: Application in sensitive electroanalysis of dobutamine

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemistry, Payame Noor University, 19395-4697 Tehran, Iran

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemistry, Faculty of Science, Shahrekord University, P.O. Box 115, Shahrekord, Iran

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Simple hydrothermal route was developed for synthesis of NiMnO3 in the presence of graphene quantum dot. The structure and morphological aspect of nano-hybrid were approved using FE-SEM, XRD and FT-IR. Then, these nano-particles were chosen as a modifier in carbon paste electrode, which produces noticeable catalytic effect for electro-oxidation of dobutamine. The oxidation mechanism of the drug was also studied using cyclic voltammetry. After variable optimization, a linear regression function was found in the range of 0.08–40.0 μM with the detection limit of 0.02 μM between the concentration of dobutamine and its anodic signal. The utility of the modified electrode was assessed by measuring the concentration of dobutamine in serum samples.

ce:section.ce:para: Dobutamine (DB) is a member of catecholamine drugs used in treating coronary heart disease and cardiogenic shock [1]. It is used to improve heart rate or cardiac output in congestive heart failure [2]. DB must be used with caution in some diseases such as atrial fibrillation because it can increase the atrioventricular conduction [3]. The structure of DB contains three functional groups consisting of a catechol, secondary amine and phenol ring with great potential for participating in redox reactions [4]. In recent years, chemically modified electrodes have found significant applications in electrochemical techniques [5, 6]. Surface modification of electrodes causes some improvements in sensitivity and reproducibility of detection and in some cases increases selectivity to analyte by immobilizing functional groups. The advent of nano-materials-based sensing systems offers a sensitive, fast and low-cost method in electrochemical detection [7–12]. Graphene quantum dots (GQDs), as a novel class of carbon-based material, have found many applications in electrode modification [13, 14]. They are a kind of 0D material [15, 16] with size diameter smaller than 20 nm. The quantum confinement effects of GQDs result in large surface area and high conductivity, which can be used as a good sensing material for construction of electrochemical sensors. Recently, metal oxide nano-particles have been applied as an efficient electrode modifier material, which mediates the electron transfers between the analyte and the electrode [17]. Mostly used electrode materials were transition metal oxides like RuO2, Co3O4 and MnO, which suffer limitation of poor electrical conductivity [18]. The use of binary metal oxides such as NiCo2O4 and Zn2SnO4 can lessen this restriction [19, 20]. NiMnO3 is one of the binary metal oxides with good electrical conductivity, which can adapt itself to ilmenite crystal type [21]. Zhu et al. synthesized the nickel manganese ilmenite-type (NiMnO3) with some characteristics such as excellent capacitance characteristics, good electric conductivity and low cost [22]. NiMnO3 has a rhombohedral structure and both cations (Ni2+ and Mn4+) occupy octahedral positions. Giri et al. prepared N-doped graphene/NiMnO3 to use in supercapacitors [23]. The π-systems and carboxyl/hydroxyl groups on the surface of graphene can simply interact with transition metal ions [24]; and in a similar manner, incorporation of NiMnO3 with GQDs would be superior if the presence of GQDs results in better conductivity or smaller size of GQDs/NiMnO3 nano-hybrid. In this study, nitrogen-doped GQDs were synthesized using a reaction between citric acid and urea. Then, GQDs/NiMnO3 nano-hybrid was prepared via a one-step hydrothermal method. The synthesized nano-composite was used as a modifier in carbon paste electrode (CPE), which causes facilitation of electron transfer process in DB oxidation and enhances sensitivity for the determination of this drug. The proposed modified electrode provides accurate analysis of DB in serum samples. To the best of our knowledge, there has been no report on the application of GQDs/NiMnO3 nano-hybrid in electrochemical sensing field.

Electroanalysis was performed using Metrohm Autolab PGSTAT101 equipped with NOVA software (Ecochemie, Utrecht, the Netherlands), and the three-electrode system contained a platinum counter electrode, an Ag/AgCl reference electrode, and the GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE as the working electrode. The XRD (Holland Philips X pert, X-ray diffractometer with Cu-Kα radiation) and FE-SEM (Hitachi S-4160, Japan) were applied for structural and morphological investigation. FT-IR analysis was recorded using JASCO FTIR-4100 spectrometer (JASCO, Japan). All chemicals were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich (Sigma-Aldrich Co., Ltd., St. Louis, MO, USA). Dobutamine hydrochloride (≥98%) was dissolved in double distilled water to prepare 1 × 10−2 mol L−1 DB stock solution. Phosphate buffer solutions (0.05 mol L−1) were prepared by mixing various ratios of Na2HPO4 and NaH2PO4 solutions, and pH levels were adjusted by adding H3PO4 and/or NaOH solution. Pure graphite powder (particle size <50 mm) and high-viscosity paraffin (d = 0.88 kg L−1) were used for the preparation of paste electrodes. Citric acid (≥99%), urea (98%), manganese(II) acetate tetrahydrate (99.99%) and nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate (99.999%) were used as precursors for the synthesis of GQDs/NiMnO3.

N-doped GQDs were synthesized by pyrolysis of citric acid [25]. GQDs/NiMnO3 nano-composites were prepared by a facile hydrothermal approach [23]. About 0.9 g urea was dissolved in 40.0 mL distilled water. Then, 0.1 g Ni(NO3)2·6H2O, 0.1674 g Mn(CH3COO)2·4H2O and 5.0 mL GQDs were added to the above mixture and it was stirred for 10 min. The mixture was put into a Teflon container sealed in steel autoclave and heated at 180 °C for 12 h. The NiMnO3 nano-particles were prepared with the same procedure without adding GQDs.

Human serums of four healthy volunteers were collected and frozen until assay. The samples were centrifuged (2500 rpm) for 5 min and then filtered through Millipore membrane filter (0.45 μm pore size). Each serum (1.0 mL) was spiked with DB. For electrochemical determination of DB, the serum was diluted 10.0 times with a 0.05 M PBS in pH = 3.0 and was transferred into the electrochemical cell. The contents of the DB in the plasma samples were determined using standard addition method.

GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE was made by mixing 800 mg of graphite powder and 50 mg of GQDs/NiMnO3 nano-composite. Then, 5 mL diethyl ether was added and the slurry was hand-mixed in a mortar. After vaporization of diethyl ether, 200 mg paraffin oil was added and mixed in a mortar and pestle to get a uniformly wetted paste. NiMnO3/CPE was prepared in the same way with these ratios: 800 mg of graphite powder, 50 mg NiMnO3, 200 mg paraffin oil and 5 mL diethyl ether. Unmodified carbon paste electrode was prepared by mixing 800 mg of graphite powder and 200 mg paraffin oil. The resulting pastes were pressed into the hole at the end of the electrode.

Synthesis of GQDs/NiMnO3 is based on electrostatic co-precipitation in which the positively charged metal ions can be attracted on negatively charged oxygen and nitrogen-containing functional groups that exist at edges and surfaces of GQDs [26]. Under hydrothermal reaction condition, decomposition of urea produces ammonia [27] and the pH of aqueous media increase (due to generation of OH− ions) which is favorable for the formation of metal oxide nanoparticles [28]. The FE-SEM of NiMnO3 NPs (Fig. 1A) illustrates a highly ordered structure arranged in a well-defined geometric shape (250 nm). While FE-SEM of GQDs/NiMnO3 nano-particles (Fig. 1B) shows that the presences of GQDs induce the formation of NiMnO3 with smaller dimensions (40 nm mean diameter). Furthermore, the cubic angled surfaces were turned into spherical shapes. Fig. 1C shows the XRD patterns of the GQDs/NiMnO3 NPs synthesized by hydrothermal method. The formation of NiMnO3 crystal was proved by major reflection Miller indices at: 002, 104, 015, 006, 113 and 202, which are in agreement with the standard XRD pattern of NiMnO3 NPs [23]. The FTIR spectra of NiMnO3 crystal (Fig. 1D) and GQDs/NiMnO3 (Fig. 1E) show two peaks at 586 and 423 cm−1, which can be assigned to NiO and MnO, respectively [29]. For GQDs/NiMnO3, the presence of broad absorption bands at 3420 cm−1, can be assigned to stretching vibrations of amino and hydroxyl groups [25] on the surface of nitrogen-doped GQDs. Furthermore, the bands at 1572 cm−1 and 1400 cm−1 are from the bending vibrations of CC and CN on the surface of GQDs, respectively [25]. At Fig. 1D, the bands at 3450 cm−1 and 1630 cm−1 can be attributed to OH stretching and bending vibrations of adsorbed water on NiMnO3 crystal.

Cyclic voltammetry (CV) response of [Fe(CN)6]3−/4− at CPE (Fig. 2A) exhibits a poor redox current with large separation for peak potential (∆Ep = 130 mV). Modification of CPE with NiMnO3 NPs causes about 1.85 fold increase in CV response because of high conductivity and nanometer response of NiMnO3 (Fig. 2B). Further modification of CPE with GQDs/NiMnO3 nano-composite leads to about 3.74-fold increase in redox current of [Fe(CN)6]3−/4− with ∆Ep = 90 mV related to CPE (Fig. 2C). The real surface areas of the bare and modified electrodes were calculated using Randles-Sevick formula using 1 mmol L−1 K3Fe(CN)6 as a probe in the 0.1 mol L−1 KCl electrolyte. From the slope of the Ipa–ν1/2 relation, the real surface area was 0.21 cm2 for the unmodified CPE, and 0.82 cm2 for GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE, which is 3.9 times greater than that for the CPE.

The charge of DB at pH below 8.0 is positive, which leads to attraction by negative charge of graphene nano-particles surface. The relation of oxidation peak current of DB and scan rate in the range 10–120 mV s−1 is linear with regression equation of I
p = 7.8ʋ + 0.0517 (R 2 = 0.998), which denotes the adsorption process (Fig. 5 ).

The calibration graph was obtained using differential pulse voltammetry (pulse amplitude of 90 mV, pulse time of 50 ms and the scan rate of 60 mV s−1) from anodic current of various DB concentrations at the GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE. The calibration plot (Fig. 6 ) was linear in the range of 0.08–40.0 μmol L −1 with the corresponding equation of I
p (μA) = (0.646 ± 0.003) C (μM) + (1.76 ± 0.100) (R 2 = 0.994, n = 5), where C is DB concentration and Ip is its oxidation response. The limit of detection (at signal-to-noise ratio of 3.0) was calculated as 0.02 μmol L −1. The comparison of the linear dynamic range and detection limit (as two important figures of merit) in this method with other electrochemical methods was given in Table 1 [1, 4, 30–33]. Except for TiO2/N/CNP/GCE as DB electrochemical sensor, our sensing system has the lowest LOD and widest dynamic range. The stability of GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE was checked by DPV over two months for 1.0 μmol L−1 of DB while being kept at room temperature. The anodic peak current was changed 3.0% from its initial value, which demonstrates good stability of modified electrode and makes it suitable for routine analysis. The reproducibility of the sensor was evaluated by calculation of relative standard deviation (RSD) of current responses (DPVs) for 1.0 μmol L−1 DB at six modified electrodes, which were constructed separately. The RSD% was found as 4.2%, which represents good reproducibility of the sensor.

The effects of co-exited species commonly found in biological samples were examined in DB detection. The largest concentration of interferences producing variation in DB current less than 3σ was defined as tolerance limit, where the σ is the standard deviation of 5.0 μmol L−1 DB current in 5 repetitive determinations. The results of this study are shown in Table 2 .

ce:section.ce:section-title: Determination of DB in spiked serum
ce:section.ce:para: Buhler et al. reported mean values of epinephrine, norepinephrine and dopamine plasma levels as 0.35, 1.2 and 0.64 nmol L−1, respectively [34]. The reported plasma concentration of DB after clinical infusion is the range of 166–630 nmol L−1 [35]. According to the reported values the minimum concentration level of DB in plasma is at least 100 times higher than the mentioned biogenic amines (three catecholamine neurotransmitters). Thus, despite significant interferences of epinephrine and dopamine, these catechol amines don't affect the oxidation signal of DB in plasma samples. Different values of DB standard solution were spiked to the four serum samples so that drug concentration could be fitted in the linear dynamic range of the technique. Spike-and-recovery method was applied to determine DB concentration. Table 3 shows the recovery percentages, which are in the reasonable range (92.0–110.0%), conforming the good analytical utility of modified electrode.

A novel and facile strategy was established for synthesis of nickel manganese oxide ilmenite-type/GQDs. Synthesis of NiMnO3 NPs in the presence of GQDs induces the formation of NiMnO3 nano-particles about six-fold smaller. Application of NiMnO3/GQD as a modifier in CPE exhibits an outstanding catalytic effect on the electro-oxidation of DB. The desirable electro-analytical features of GQDs/NiMnO3/CPE included high sensitivity, stability and reproducibility along with technical simplicity in addition to fast preparation of modified electrode. The modified electrode can be applied as a simple and useful protocol for determination of DB in spiked serum samples with reasonable recoveries.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The authors gratefully acknowledge support of this work by the Research Council of Payame Noor University and Green Chemistry (27281).
